basics.name: Shuhan Zheng
basics.summary: M.Eng. graduate from the University of Toronto with experience streamlining complex analytical problem solutions through first-principles approaches, physics-aware software development, nonlinear dynamical systems, computation-intensive problems, multidisciplinary engineering, data-driven work, electronics, mechanical prototyping, and systems-level analysis.

education.institution: University of Toronto Institute for Aerospace Studies

work.company: University of Toronto Institute for Aerospace Studies, Aerospace Mechatronics Lab
work.summary: Large data-enabled space-domain awareness research on temporarily bound objects and mission-viable asteroids.
highlights: Abstract accepted by AMOS 2026 as first author: Dichotomizing Temporarily Bound Objects Using Capture States.
Used statistical modeling and probabilistic neural networks to enable rapid and early detection of mission-viable asteroids.

work.company: University of Toronto Near Field Cosmology Group
work.summary: Automated stellar data retrieval, sanitizing, cataloging, and preparation for machine-learning ingestion.
highlights: Built a turnkey solution with a portable interface for machine-learning model ingestion.
Project adopted by the Near Field Cosmology Group; the resulting databases support subsequent publications.

work.company: China Spallation Neutron Source, Chinese Academy of Sciences
work.summary: Developed deterministic simulation software for polarized-neutron imaging of magnetized samples.
highlights: Independently developed a Python-based deterministic simulation tool for generating synthetic polarized-neutron images of magnetized samples.
Reduced computational time from days to hours compared with conventional Monte Carlo methods.

work.summary: Space systems work for the FINCH microsatellite mission.
highlights: Implemented and tested image-correction algorithms for hyperspectral remote-sensing data in collaboration with the optics team.
Co-authored team design papers for the 2022 and 2023 Small Satellite Conferences as a member of the FINCH mission team.

work.company: University of Toronto Physics Student Union
highlights: Managed the organization, logistics, and coordination of outreach, fundraising, social, and academic events.
Acted as a liaison between faculty and students.
Assisted the launch OPS and Safety team by enforcing launch pad clearance and compliance with launch safety regulations.

portfolio.name: AMAT - Agentic Mission Analysis Toolbox
portfolio.summary: AI harness and toolbox for reliable application in spaceflight mission design, converting user intent into mission-ready spacecraft flight plans with interactive visualization for human-in-the-loop verification and validation.

portfolio.name: GMAT Automation for Asteroid Trajectory Prediction
portfolio.summary: Monte Carlo simulation of real asteroid trajectories for prediction of future Earth encounters with uncertainties; predictions verified against published journal literature.

portfolio.name: Catalog Acquisition and Compilation Tool (CACT)
portfolio.summary: Autonomous data retrieval, sanitizing, and management pipeline with user interface API for hierarchical and relational scientific data.

portfolio.name: Polarization-modulated Optical Communication Demonstrator
portfolio.summary: Designed and built a prototype for serial transmission of binary information via laser link.

publications.name: FINCH: A Blueprint for Accessible and Scientifically Valuable Remote Sensing Satellite Missions
publications.summary: Miles, A., Maranto, D., Xu, S., et al., Zheng, S., et al. Weekend Session 7: Science/Mission Payloads - Research & Academia II, Utah State University, Logan, UT.

publications.name: Beyond the Visible: Jointly Attending to Spectral and Spatial Dimensions with HSI-Diffusion for the FINCH Spacecraft
publications.summary: Vyse, I., Dagli, R., Chadha, D. V., Ma, J. P., Chen, H., et al., Zheng, S., & Nguyen, K. Session X: Ground Systems, Utah State University, Logan, UT.
